channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TI-Kenya Report Highlights Elite Influence Weakening Leadership Standards
item.description: A recent report by Transparency International Kenya (TI-Kenya) has revealed a big gap between Kenya's constitutional ideals on leadership and integrity and what actually happens in politics. The repor...

item.title: NIDCOM Urges Mozambique to Release 42 Nigerians Arrested Without Charges
item.description: The Nigerians in Diaspora Commission (NIDCOM), under the leadership of Hon. Abike Dabiri-Erewa, don raise alarm over the arrest of 42 Nigerians for Mozambique. According to NIDCOM, these people, wey d...

item.title: Government reduces rents and grants tax relief to support new Kariakoo Market traders
item.description: The Tanzanian government has taken steps to support traders at the new Kariakoo Market by reducing rents and offering tax relief. The initiative, directed by President Dr. Samia Suluhu Hassan during t...

item.title: Queensland Researchers Develop Quick Screening Tool for Early Endometriosis Detection
item.description: Queensland researchers have developed a five-minute test that could drastically reduce the time it takes to diagnose endometriosis, a condition affecting roughly one in seven Australian women. Current...

item.title: Rivers State Trains Adolescent Girls to End FGM Practice
item.description: The Permanent Secretary of Rivers State Ministry of Social Welfare and Rehabilitation, Mrs. Lauretta Davies-Dimkpa, don yan say the success of the 12-day Female Genital Mutilation (FGM) elimination pr...

item.title: President Marcos emphasizes importance of women’s education at UN session
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. highlighted the critical role of educating women during the 70th session of the United Nations Commission on the Status of Women in New York. He described Fil...

item.title: Insights from a Personal Trainer: Salary Expectations, Fitness Advice, and Key Supplements
item.description: Luke Worthington, a personal trainer specializing in physical preparation for TV and film, shares insights into the profession, including salary ranges, training philosophies, and essential supplement...

item.title: Provincial Governments in Pakistan Implement Fuel Conservation Measures Amid Global Oil Shortage from Middle East Conflict
item.description: On March 9, 2026, the governments of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP), Punjab, and Balochistan announced urgent fuel-saving steps in response to the global oil crisis sparked by the US-Israel war on Iran. In K...

item.title: 15-year-old Oxford United goalkeeper dies after collapsing during match
item.description: Amelia Aplin, a 15-year-old goalkeeper for Oxford United's academy, tragically passed away after collapsing during a Junior Premier League match against Fulham at the club’s training centre in Horspat...

item.title: Johannesburg electricity billing corruption: Two contractors arrested in entrapment operation
item.description: In Johannesburg, two suspects were arrested following an entrapment operation where a contractor allegedly offered to reduce or reverse a resident's municipal electricity debt of over R114 000 in exch...

item.title: PM Shehbaz announces austerity plan amid global fuel crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Monday outlined a broad austerity plan to tackle the impact of the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict on Pakistan’s energy supply and economy. With fuel supply disrupted ...

item.title: Middle East faces growing risks to water infrastructure amid regional conflicts
item.description: Attacks on water infrastructure in the Middle East are becoming a concerning new aspect of regional conflicts. Recently, Bahrain reported that an Iranian drone strike targeted a desalination plant, al...

item.title: Kerala Government Launches Programme to Recognise Responsible and Civic-Minded Students
item.description: The Kerala government has announced that students demonstrating civic consciousness and social responsibility will be honoured. This statement was made by the Minister for General Education, V. Sivank...

item.title: Australia’s Strategic Options: Balancing US Reliance and Regional Partnerships with Canada
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney recently warned the Australian parliament that the post-war global order is fragmenting, leaving middle powers like Canada and Australia vulnerable due to their clo...

item.title: Rising Company Insolvencies in New Zealand Prompt Review of Directors’ Duties
item.description: New Zealand has seen a significant rise in corporate insolvencies, reaching the highest levels in 15 years, with many companies entering liquidation or formal processes in 2025. This surge has coincid...

item.title: Impact of Iran Conflict on Gulf Engagement in the Horn of Africa
item.description: Over di last ten years, Gulf countries like UAE and Qatar don dey heavily involved for di Horn of Africa, including Sudan, Ethiopia, Eritrea, Somaliland, Somalia and Djibouti. Dem dey provide money, a...

item.title: As Ever brand clarifies speculation over international expansion following Netflix split
item.description: Meghan Markle’s lifestyle brand, As Ever, has addressed recent speculation about an expansion into Australia, clarifying that no decisions have been made regarding international growth. ITV News recen...

item.title: Government Imposes Austerity Steps to Counter Fuel Crisis from Middle East Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif addressed the nation on March 9, 2026, announcing a series of austerity measures to tackle the economic fallout from the US-Israel war on Iran, which has driven global oi...

item.title: IT Company Confirms Compliance with LTO Concession Amid Graft Allegations
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Stradcom Corp., an information technology firm, has reiterated that its operations with the Land Transportation Office (LTO) are conducted under a valid and legally binding conce...

item.title: Controversial haunted house cold open from The Office's 'Koi Pond' episode removed after initial broadcast due to suicide depiction
item.description: A deleted cold open from the season 6 episode 'Koi Pond' of the US version of The Office, which first aired on 29 October 2009, has resurfaced in discussions after being shared online. The scene depic...
